rules.title: Target Azure region is not evidenced
rules.failureMessage: The submitted evidence does not clearly identify the Azure target region or region-pair strategy.
rules.recommendation: Document target Azure regions and region-pair assumptions. See https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/well-architected/reliability/regions-availability-zones.

rules.title: Availability-zone design is not evidenced
rules.failureMessage: The design does not evidence availability zones, zone redundancy, or an explicit non-zone requirement.
rules.recommendation: Document zone redundancy or justify why it is not required. See https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/well-architected/reliability/regions-availability-zones.

rules.title: Identity and access model is not evidenced
rules.failureMessage: The submission does not evidence an Entra ID, RBAC, managed identity, PIM, or privileged access model.
rules.recommendation: Document identity, RBAC, managed identity, and privileged access controls. See https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/well-architected/security/identity-access.

rules.title: Privileged access controls are not evidenced
rules.failureMessage: The design does not evidence PIM, privileged role governance, or least-privilege access review.
rules.recommendation: Add privileged access management and least-privilege role review. See https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/well-architected/security/identity-access.

rules.title: Internet-facing boundary control is not evidenced
rules.failureMessage: The design appears to include public or internet-facing services without evidenced WAF, firewall, Application Gateway, Front Door, APIM, or NSG boundary control.
rules.recommendation: Document boundary protection for public entry points using WAF, Azure Firewall, Application Gateway, Front Door, APIM, or NSGs. See https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/well-architected/security/networking.

rules.title: Private connectivity is not evidenced
rules.failureMessage: The design does not evidence private endpoints, private DNS, VNet integration, or equivalent private connectivity for platform services.
rules.recommendation: Document private connectivity and DNS design for platform services. See https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/well-architected/security/networking.

rules.title: Backup and disaster recovery strategy is not evidenced
rules.failureMessage: The submission does not evidence backup, restore, DR, RTO, or RPO commitments.
rules.recommendation: Document backup, restore testing, DR pattern, RTO, and RPO. See https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/well-architected/reliability/backup-restore.

rules.title: Health and failover behavior is not evidenced
rules.failureMessage: The design does not evidence health probes, retry behavior, failover, or resilience testing.
rules.recommendation: Document health probes, retry policies, failover, and resilience test plans. See https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/well-architected/reliability/.

rules.title: Monitoring and alerting are not evidenced
rules.failureMessage: The submission does not evidence Azure Monitor, Log Analytics, Application Insights, alerting, or operational dashboards.
rules.recommendation: Document monitoring, logs, alerts, dashboards, and ownership. See https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/well-architected/operational-excellence/monitoring.

rules.title: Infrastructure-as-code and CI/CD are not evidenced
rules.failureMessage: The design does not evidence Terraform, Bicep, ARM, CI/CD, deployment automation, or runbooks.
rules.recommendation: Document IaC, CI/CD, release controls, and runbooks. See https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/well-architected/operational-excellence/infrastructure-as-code.
